description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white to light gray coloration, featuring rough, uneven surfaces and natural crevices. The rock has a somewhat lumpy and bulbous form, with various protrusions and indentations that give it a rugged appearance. Its surface texture is coarse and granular, with visible patches of dirt or mineral staining in some areas. A small, rectangular plaque or label is affixed to one side, suggesting it may have been cataloged or identified for a specific purpose. The rock appears solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall look is consistent with a boulder that might be used as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A large, rough, white-gray rock with an irregular, lumpy shape and a small plaque attached to one side. Its coarse surface and solid form suggest it is used as a barrier or decorative element.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broadest face of the rock, with a small rectangular plaque clearly visible on its surface.
description_view_1: This side view reveals a more tapered end, with pronounced surface roughness and fewer visible markings.
description_view_2: The rock appears elongated and squat from this angle, highlighting its uneven, jagged edges and natural indentations.
description_view_3: This rear view shows a rounded, less detailed surface with a generally uniform texture and no visible plaque.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
attribute_integration_with_urban_infrast: A plaque attached to the rock suggests intentional placement or cataloging, possibly as a barrier or decorative feature.
